qtype: multi-part problem solving with percentages, money, and bounds
tex:
(a) A shop sells dress fabric for \$2.97 per metre.
\newline

(i) (b) A customer buys 9 metres of this fabric.
\newline

Calculate the change he receives from \$50.
\newline

\$ 

(c) The selling price of \$2.97 per metre is an increase of 8\% on the cost price.
\newline

Calculate the cost price.
\newline

\$  per metre

(d) A dressmaker charges \$35 or 2300 rupees to make a dress.
\newline

Calculate the difference in price when the exchange rate is 1 rupee = \$0.0153.
\newline
Give your answer in rupees.
\newline

 rupees

(e) The dressmaker measures a length of fabric as 600 m, correct to the nearest 5 metres.
\newline
He cuts this into dress lengths of 9 m, correct to the nearest metre.
\newline

Calculate the largest number of complete dress lengths he could cut.
\newline

content.content:
A shop sells dress fabric for \$2.97 per metre.
\newline

A customer buys 9 metres of this fabric.
\newline

Calculate the change he receives from \$50.
\newline

\$ \AnswerLine[][]

The selling price of \$2.97 per metre is an increase of 8\% on the cost price.
\newline

Calculate the cost price.
\newline

\$ \AnswerLine[][] per metre

A dressmaker charges \$35 or 2300 rupees to make a dress.
\newline

Calculate the difference in price when the exchange rate is 1 rupee = \$0.0153.
\newline
Give your answer in rupees.
\newline

The dressmaker measures a length of fabric as 600 m, correct to the nearest 5 metres.
\newline
He cuts this into dress lengths of 9 m, correct to the nearest metre.
\newline

Calculate the largest number of complete dress lengths he could cut.
\newline

Calculate the area of the square.
\newline

Calculate the area of the shaded segment.
\newline

Calculate the perimeter of the shaded segment.
\newline

stem.content:
The vertices of a square ABCD lie on the circumference of a circle, radius 8 cm.
\newline

qtype: graphing functions, solving equations and inequalities graphically
tex:
The table shows some values for $y = 2x + \dfrac{1}{x} - 3$ for $0.125 \leq x \leq 3$.
\newline

\begin{InsertScreenShot}
    [Diagram]
}
    }
\end{InsertScreenShot}

(a) Complete the table.
\newline

(b) On the grid, draw the graph of $y = 2x + \dfrac{1}{x} - 3$ for $0.125 \leq x \leq 3$.
\newline

(c) Use your graph to solve $2x + \dfrac{1}{x} - 3 \geq 2$.
\newline

(d) The equation $\dfrac{1}{x} = 7 - 3x$ can be solved using your graph in part (b) and a straight line.
\newline

(i) (e) Write down the equation of this straight line.
\newline

(f) Draw this straight line and solve the equation $\dfrac{1}{x} = 7 - 3x$.
\newline

 or

Complete the table.
\newline

On the grid, draw the graph of $y = 2x + \dfrac{1}{x} - 3$ for $0.125 \leq x \leq 3$.
\newline

Use your graph to solve $2x + \dfrac{1}{x} - 3 \geq 2$.
\newline

The equation $\dfrac{1}{x} = 7 - 3x$ can be solved using your graph in part (b) and a straight line.

qtype: rearranging formula, factorisation, simplifying and adding algebraic fractions, solving compound inequality
tex:
(a) Make $t$ the subject of the formula $s = k - t^2$.
\newline

(b) Factorise $x^2 - 25$.
\newline

(c) Simplify $\dfrac{x^2 - 25}{x^2 - 2x - 35}$.
\newline

(d) Write as a single fraction in its simplest form.
\newline

$\dfrac{x-8}{x} + \dfrac{3x}{x+1}$

(e) Find the integer values of $n$ that satisfy the inequality.
\newline

$18 - 2n \leq 6 \leq 18 - 2n + 12$

content.content:
Make $t$ the subject of the formula $s = k - t^2$.
\newline

Factorise $x^2 - 25$.
\newline

Simplify $\dfrac{x^2 - 25}{x^2 - 2x - 35}$.
\newline

Write as a single fraction in its simplest form.
\newline

$\dfrac{x-8}{x} + \dfrac{3x}{x+1}$

Find the integer values of $n$ that satisfy the inequality.
\newline

$18 - 2n \leq 6 \leq 18 - 2n + 12$

tex:
Klaus buys $x$ silver balloons and $y$ gold balloons for a party.
\newline

He buys
more gold balloons than silver balloons
at least 15 silver balloons
less than 50 gold balloons
a total of no more than 70 balloons.
\newline
(a) Write down four inequalities, in terms of $x$ and/or $y$, to show this information.
\newline

(b) On the grid, show the information from part (a) by drawing four straight lines and shading the
unwanted regions.
\newline

\begin{InsertScreenShot}
    [Diagram]
}
    }
\end{InsertScreenShot}

(c) Silver balloons cost \$2 and gold balloons cost \$3.
\newline

Calculate the most that Klaus could spend.
\newline

\$

content.content:
Write down four inequalities, in terms of $x$ and/or $y$, to show this information.
\newline

On the grid, show the information from part (a) by drawing four straight lines and shading the
unwanted regions.
\newline

Silver balloons cost \$2 and gold balloons cost \$3.
\newline

Calculate the most that Klaus could spend.
\newline

\$ \AnswerLine[][]

\newline

He buys

items: more gold balloons than silver balloons
a total of no more than 70 balloons.

tex:
The frequency table shows information about the time, $m$ minutes, that each of 160 people spend in a library.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
Time ($m$ minutes) & $0 \leq m < 10$ & $10 \leq m < 40$ & $40 \leq m < 60$ & $60 \leq m < 90$ & $90 \leq m < 100$ & $100 \leq m < 120$ \\
\hline
Frequency & 3 & 39 & 43 & 55 & 11 & 9 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

(a) Find the probability that one of these people, chosen at random, spends more than 100 minutes in
the library.
\newline

(b) Calculate an estimate of the mean time spent in the library.
\newline

 minutes

(c) Complete the cumulative frequency table below.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
Time ($m$ minutes) & $m < 10$ & $m < 40$ & $m < 60$ & $m < 90$ & $m < 100$ & $m < 120$ \\
\hline
Cumulative frequency & 3 & 42 & & & & \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

(d) On the grid opposite, draw the cumulative frequency diagram.
\newline

\begin{InsertScreenShot}
    [Diagram]
}
    }
\end{InsertScreenShot}

(e) Use your cumulative frequency diagram to find

(i) (f) the median,

 minutes

(g) the interquartile range,

 minutes

(h) the 90th percentile,

 minutes

(i) the number of people who spend more than 30 minutes in the library.
\newline

qtype: Vectors and Matrices (Not an algebra subtopic)
tex:
(a) Find the magnitude of the vector $\begin{pmatrix} -1 \\ 7 \end{pmatrix}$.
\newline

(b) The determinant of the matrix $\begin{pmatrix} 6 & 2m \\ 5 & m \end{pmatrix}$ is 24.
\newline

Find the value of $m$.
\newline

(c) $L = \begin{pmatrix} 2 & 5 \end{pmatrix}$, $M = \begin{pmatrix} -4 \\ 2 \end{pmatrix}$, $N = \begin{pmatrix} 3 & 9 \end{pmatrix}$.
\newline

Work out the following.
\newline

(i) (d) $NM$

(e) $LM$

(f) $L^2$

(g) $L^{-1}$

content.content:
Find the magnitude of the vector $\begin{pmatrix} -1 \\ 7 \end{pmatrix}$.
\newline

\newline

$L = \begin{pmatrix} 2 & 5 \end{pmatrix}$, $M = \begin{pmatrix} -4 \\ 2 \end{pmatrix}$, $N = \begin{pmatrix} 3 & 9 \end{pmatrix}$.
\newline

Work out the following.
\newline
$NM$
$LM$
